content: Hi, I'm Sutton. I've been working as a DevOps engineer in a company in New York for a few years now, and I'm looking to meet new people who share my interests.
Nice to meet you, Sutton! What kind of social events are you interested in attending or hosting?
I'm really into home automation, so maybe a smart home meetup or event. I've also been getting into skiing and aquariums, so maybe a group that combines those interests.
Home automation is a fascinating field. Have you thought of joining a local smart home group or attending any smart home-related events in New York?
Yeah, I've been looking into it. I'd love to meet other people who share my passion for home automation. And maybe we can even plan a ski trip, I've been enjoying hitting the slopes in Vermont.
Vermont is a great place to ski. What kind of skiing are you into? Downhill, cross-country, or something else?
I'm really into downhill skiing, I love the speed and thrill of it. And yeah, I was thinking of hosting a game night, maybe with some chess and rust programming activities.
Chess is a great game. What kind of rust programming projects are you interested in working on? And have you thought of any specific dates for the game night?
I'm really into building smart home devices and automating tasks. And yeah, I was thinking of having the game night on Saturday, March 12th.
That sounds like a great idea. Have you thought of any other activities or games to play at the game night?
Yeah, I was thinking of setting up a few aquariums and having a mini tournament. And maybe we can even have a skiing-themed challenge.
That sounds like a fantastic idea! A skiing-themed challenge can be a lot of fun. How many people are you planning to invite?
Around 15-20 people, including my colleague, Jack, who's also into home automation and skiing.
That's a great turnout. I'll make sure to remind you to follow up with Jack about the event.
Thanks, I appreciate it. I'm really looking forward to the game night and catching up with my friends. And I'm also excited to spend time with my new parrot, Milo, he's a lot of fun to be around.
